summary: Frontend regression after animations refactor (Reveal + AnimatePresence PaymentModal + hero float). All 7 requested flows pass on desktop 1920x4000 and mobile 390x844.
verified_flows: Store loads with 6 ranks, 5 keys, 6 bundles (17 buy buttons total, matches expectation)
keys-currency-usd toggles prices to $ inside Reveal-wrapped section header
keys-sort-asc + ranks-sort-desc clicks execute successfully within motion.div
buy-habibi opens payment-modal (AnimatePresence); QR image loaded (complete=true, naturalWidth=680); UPI id 'shiekhjeet19@fam' present
close-payment-btn closes modal (count=0 after 1.5s exit animation)
Overlay click on payment-modal also closes it (count=0)
players-online element present, shows '0/999' (client-side fetch from api.mcsrvstat.us worked)
Mobile: mobile-menu-btn opens menu, mobile-refund-link navigates to /refund, refund-48hr block renders
notes: First test used 600ms wait post-close which was shorter than AnimatePresence exit; re-test with 1500ms confirmed modal fully unmounts. No bug.
Console shows some 401 errors from unrelated resource load (likely /api/session or preview auth ping); does not affect any user flow tested.
Hero logo float + Reveal scroll-in animations render smoothly; no layout shift observed.
critical_code_review_comments: Overlay-click-to-close relies on comparing e.target to overlay ref; works fine, but consider e.target===e.currentTarget pattern for clarity if not already.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Animation regression passed. If retesting, allow >=1000ms after close for AnimatePresence exit before asserting modal removal. Live status API can take up to 5s.
